sections.paragraph:
τριετία, ας, ἡ (τριέτης; Theophr. et al.; Plut., Lucian; Artem. 4, 1 p. 202, 9; OGI 669, 35; pap; Jos., Ant. 19, 351.—B-D-F §5, 3) a period of three years, three years Ac 20:18 D, 31.—M-M.

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1015
